steps.title: Translate to equations
steps.content:
We are given two sentences that translate directly into the system of equations.

First sentence: The sum of a number x and 7 is twice as large as another number y.

This translates to $x + 7 = 2y$. Second sentence: The number y is 3 less than the number x, so this can be expressed as $y = x - 3$.

steps.title: Identify correct system
steps.content: Looking at our derived equations, we match them against each option. Our two equations are $x + 7 = 2y$ and $y = x - 3$, which directly correspond to options given.

steps.title: Conclude with answer
steps.content: The system $x + 7 = 2y$ and $y = x - 3$ is listed as A in the choices. That matches choice A — A is correct.
